specific_test: Neighbor-similarity permutation: do direct GRN edges (regulator-effector pairs) show more correlated RELAX K than non-adjacent panel-gene pairs, degree-preserving rewiring null
inputs_needed: signed directed GRN edge list; per-gene pooled RELAX K
n_available: 13 panel genes form the connected induced subgraph; 15 non-self directed edges / 15 unique undirected pairs (168-node core GRN); 18 edges in 803-node substrate sensitivity check
blocking_gaps: none for pooled K; per-branch aBSREL version blocked by empty JSONs (see row below)

specific_test: Regulator-vs-effector split: MITF/SOX10/PAX3 (source in edges) K distribution vs their direct targets' K distribution
inputs_needed: signed directed GRN edge list; per-gene pooled RELAX K
n_available: 3 regulator genes (MITF,SOX10,PAX3) vs 10 distinct target genes reached by their outgoing edges
blocking_gaps: none; purely descriptive/exploratory, n too small for a confirmatory test

specific_test: Same neighbor-similarity permutation using per-branch aBSREL signal (foreground-branch max LRT) instead of pooled K
inputs_needed: aBSREL per-branch JSON for all within-panel-edge genes
n_available: only 7 of 13 within-panel-edge genes have non-empty ABSREL.json (EDNRB,KIT,MITF,MLANA,OCA2,SOX10,TYRP1); DCT,KITLG,MC1R,PAX3,PMEL,TYR are 0-byte/failed runs
compute_location: local (if re-run) / cluster (to regenerate missing JSONs)
est_runtime: re-run missing aBSREL: ~10-60 min per gene depending on n_tips (absrel_full_panel_array.sbatch, 4h wall cap)
blocking_gaps: 6 of 13 connected genes have no aBSREL output at all; would need to re-run absrel_full_panel_array.sbatch for those genes before any per-branch topology test is possible

specific_test: Diffuse aBSREL per-branch selection signal across GRN edges to find a selected sub-network (e.g. HotNet2/heat-diffusion style)
inputs_needed: aBSREL per-branch signal for ALL/most panel genes; a connected network with >>13 scored nodes
n_available: only 13 panel genes are network-connected at all; only 7 of those have aBSREL signal; propagation needs a much larger connected, scored substrate than we have
blocking_gaps: network too sparse in panel genes (13 nodes) and selection scores too sparse (7 nodes) for meaningful diffusion; would require expression-free assignment of scores to the full 803-node substrate, which we don't have

specific_test: Pairwise mirrortree/ERC: correlate relative evolutionary rate vectors between gene pairs across the shared species tree; test GRN-edge pairs vs random pairs
inputs_needed: codon-aware alignments (have, aln117_codon.tar.gz); per-gene ML branch-length trees on common topology (need to build, script exists: 04_rerconverge.R uses phangorn); species tree (have)
n_available: 77 genes with alignments -> 2926 possible gene pairs; only 15-18 are true within-panel GRN edges (same small true-edge count as Tier1, but each pair now compared via a full rate VECTOR instead of one scalar K)
est_runtime: ~10-30 min/gene tree-building x 77 genes (array), then <30 min local/single-node aggregation
blocking_gaps: per-gene ML branch-length trees not yet built (04_rerconverge.R has not been run to produce report/rer_results.csv); needs phangorn tree fit per gene before any pairwise rate correlation is possible

specific_test: PolySel/SUMSTAT on GRN-TOPOLOGY-defined modules (e.g. TF-core: MITF/SOX10/PAX3 vs receptor: KIT/KITLG/EDNRB/MC1R vs structural/enzymatic: TYR/TYRP1/DCT/PMEL/MLANA) rather than the existing hormone-vs-pigmentation panel split
inputs_needed: 05_polysel_geneset.py (exists); per-gene RELAX/aBSREL score; a topology-derived module assignment for scored genes
n_available: only the 13 network-connected panel genes can be assigned to a GRN-derived module with any topological justification; script's SUMSTAT null (label permutation) needs a reasonably sized background -- 13 genes across 3 candidate modules (3-5 genes per module) is too small for a stable resampling null
compute_location: local (script is lightweight; existing 05_polysel_geneset.py) -- would not need cluster time itself, but is UNDERPOWERED given n
blocking_gaps: script already exists and could run today, but with only 13 scored+connected genes split across 3 modules the SUMSTAT permutation background is too thin to trust; DUPLICATES the module_selection/ hormone-vs-pigmentation SUMSTAT question in spirit (different set definition, same statistical fragility) -- deprioritized vs the coevolution test above

specific_test: Extend within-panel edge test to independent dichromatism origins: does the neighbor-similarity signal hold per-origin (14 independent origins), giving convergence evidence rather than one pooled test
inputs_needed: per-origin RELAX K for all panel genes across all 14 origins
n_available: per-origin RELAX (02c_per_origin_relax.py) has been run for only 3 of 14 origins so far (origin_7 n=74 genes, origin_8 n=64, origin_14 n=57 -- results/perorigin_v1/per_origin_K.csv); of the 13 network-connected genes, all 3 completed origins have K for most of them, but 11 origins are missing entirely
compute_location: cluster (per_origin_relax_array.sbatch already exists) to fill the remaining 11 origins
est_runtime: ~1h per origin x gene array (same shape as relax_array.sbatch, ~1h/task, array size = n genes x remaining origins)
blocking_gaps: 11 of 14 origins never run; without them, cannot ask whether the topology-selection covariance is a convergent, independent-origin signal (the statistically strongest version of the claim) versus a single pooled artifact
